characters.name: Carruthers
characters.description: Young Foreign Office clerk, bored with London society during the off-season, narrator of the story. Receives an unexpected letter from Davies inviting him to go sailing.

characters.name: Arthur Davies
characters.description: Amateur sailor who owns the yacht Dolorosa, earnest and unpretentious. Sends an urgent and somewhat mysterious invitation to Carruthers to join him cruising in the Baltic.

voice_profile.tone: measured and ironic with self-deprecating wit

settings.name: London
settings.description: Carruthers' flat and club in London, where he languishes during the social off-season.

settings.name: The yacht / Baltic Sea
settings.description: Davies' small yacht and the Baltic/North Sea sailing grounds, the anticipated destination.

plot_threads.thread: Carruthers debating whether to accept Davies' unexpected invitation to go sailing
Social snobbery versus the appeal of adventure — Carruthers weighing his London life against the unknown
Mysterious undertone to Davies' urgent invitation suggesting something beyond a casual cruise

world_rules: Edwardian social hierarchy and the importance of the London social season
Small-boat sailing in the North Sea and Baltic as a gentleman's pursuit
